evidence.content: The Great Depression was a devastating and prolonged economic recession that followed the crash of the United States stock market in 1929. The Great Depression: The greatest and longest economic recession in modern world history that ran between 1929 and 1941.:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/great_depression.asp-Final-8258581e56b6472fb8e1f12c3766187e.jpg). They include the stock market crash of 1929, the gold standard, a drop in lending, tariffs, banking panics, and contractionary monetary policies of the Fed. The stock market crash likely triggered the decade-long economic downturn, but most historians and economists agree that it didn't cause the Great Depression by itself. World War II created international trading channels and reversed burdensome price and wage controls, which helped the country recover from the Great Depression. The Great Depression taught economists, lawmakers, and the Fed how to better deal with the various economic circumstances and taught people how to survive in hard times. stock market chart:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/GettyImages-496030068-29ec863cca36413ab111277d250fe964.jpg). Stock Market Crash:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/Stock-Market-Crash-56a093a93df78cafdaa2dacf.jpg). A digital composite image of stock market data.:max_bytes(150000):strip_icc()/GettyImages-1069345714-fbda50660ce74c26944a2b03d6482473.jpg).

additional.query: Synthesize the findings from steps 1-3 to identify a consensus among historians and economists on the main causes of the Great Depression.

evidence.content: By 1929, a perfect storm of unlucky factors led to the start of the worst economic downturn in U.S. history. Things were so bad that of all the days of unemployment experienced by individual American workers in American history, half occurred during the Great Depression, according to University of California, Irvine economics Professor Gary Richardson, who has done extensive research on that period and the subject of downturns in general. “For something to be as bad as the Great Depression, you really need multiple things going wrong, in the U.S. and around the world,” Richardson says. “The stock market crash did two things,” explains Mary Eschelbach Hansen, a professor of economics at American University. As Richardson notes, the U.S. economy didn’t again reach full employment until 1940—just in time for World War II to disrupt consumption with rationing needed to ensure that the military had enough resources.

additional.query: Synthesize the findings from steps 1-3 to identify a consensus among historians and economists on the main causes of the Great Depression.

Things were so bad that of all the days of unemployment experienced by individual American workers in American history, half occurred during the Great Depression, according to University of California, Irvine economics Professor Gary Richardson, who has done extensive research on that period and the subject of downturns in general. “For something to be as bad as the Great Depression, you really need multiple things going wrong, in the U.S. and around the world,” Richardson says. “The stock market crash did two things,” explains Mary Eschelbach Hansen, a professor of economics at American University. As Richardson notes, the U.S. economy didn’t again reach full employment until 1940—just in time for World War II to disrupt consumption with rationing needed to ensure that the military had enough resources.

source.raw_snippet: By 1929, a perfect storm of unlucky factors led to the start of the worst economic downturn in U.S. history. Things were so bad that of all the days of unemployment experienced by individual American workers in American history, half occurred during the Great Depression, according to University of California, Irvine economics Professor Gary Richardson, who has done extensive research on that period and the subject of downturns in general. “For something to be as bad as the Great Depression, you really need multiple things going wrong, in the U.S. and around the world,” Richardson says. “The stock market crash did two things,” explains Mary Eschelbach Hansen, a professor of economics at American University. As Richardson notes, the U.S. economy didn’t again reach full employment until 1940—just in time for World War II to disrupt consumption with rationing needed to ensure that the military had enough resources.
source.title: 5 Causes of the Great Depression - History.com

experiment.hypothesis: The frozen v0 ASAR pipeline can produce grounded single-goal research artifacts.
experiment.name: asar-v0-what-were-the-main-causes-of-the-great-d

goal: What were the main causes of the Great Depression?

plan.goal: What were the main causes of the Great Depression?

steps.description: Search for historical accounts and primary sources from the time period (1929-1939) that describe the economic conditions leading up to the Great Depression.
steps.expected_output: A collection of relevant articles, books, or government reports that provide insight into the causes of the Great Depression.
steps.success_criteria: Identification of at least 5 key events or factors mentioned in multiple sources as contributing to the Great Depression.

steps.description: Analyze and categorize the identified causes from step 1 into broad categories (e.g., economic, political, social).
steps.expected_output: A list of categorized causes with a brief description of each.
steps.success_criteria: Presence of at least 3 distinct categories with a minimum of 2 causes per category.

steps.description: Search for scholarly articles and academic research papers that investigate the Great Depression, focusing on the identified causes from step 2.
steps.expected_output: A set of relevant academic sources that provide in-depth analysis or empirical evidence related to the causes of the Great Depression.
steps.success_criteria: Identification of at least 3 studies that support or contradict the categorized causes from step 2.

steps.description: Synthesize the findings from steps 1-3 to identify a consensus among historians and economists on the main causes of the Great Depression.
steps.expected_output: A concise summary of the most widely accepted causes, along with any notable disagreements or debates in the field.
steps.success_criteria: Presence of a clear and well-supported conclusion that addresses the goal.
